title: Getting Ready for School
summary: A mum helps her young son get ready for school in the morning. They talk about what he needs to do and what is in his lunch box.

body.speaker: Mum
body.text: Good morning, Tom! Time to wake up.

body.speaker: Son
body.text: Good morning, Mum. Do I have to get up now?

body.speaker: Mum
body.text: Yes! School starts in one hour. Go and wash your face first.

body.speaker: Son
body.text: Okay. Can I have breakfast after?

body.speaker: Mum
body.text: Of course. I am making toast and eggs for you.

body.speaker: Son
body.text: Yay! I love eggs. Mum, where is my school uniform?

body.speaker: Mum
body.text: It is on the chair in your room. I ironed it last night.

body.speaker: Son
body.text: Thank you! What is in my lunch box today?

body.speaker: Mum
body.text: There is a tuna sandwich, some grapes, and a small juice box.

body.speaker: Son
body.text: Can I have a biscuit too?

body.speaker: Mum
body.text: Yes, I put two biscuits in your bag. Now hurry up!

body.speaker: Son
body.text: Okay, okay. Mum, I cannot find my school shoes.

body.speaker: Mum
body.text: They are by the front door. You left them there yesterday.

body.speaker: Son
body.text: Oh yes! I remember now. I am ready, Mum!

body.speaker: Mum
body.text: Great! Have a good day at school. I love you.

body.speaker: Son
body.text: Love you too, Mum. See you later!

annotations.exampleEn: She knocked on the door and said, 'Time to wake up!'
Do I have to finish all the vegetables?
He put on his school uniform and packed his bag.
She ironed his shirt so it looked neat for the interview.
His lunch box had a sandwich and a piece of fruit.
She made a tuna sandwich for her son's lunch.
He packed a small bag of grapes as a healthy snack.
She gave her daughter a juice box to drink with her lunch.
Hurry up or we will be late for the bus!
He left his umbrella by the front door.

grammarNotes.title: Have to – Expressing obligation
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Have to' (phải) dùng để nói về việc bắt buộc phải làm. 'Do I have to get up now?' = Tôi có phải thức dậy bây giờ không? Ở hiện tại: 'I have to go', 'She has to study', 'Do you have to work?'. Khác với 'must' (mang ý nghĩa ép buộc mạnh hơn), 'have to' thường dùng cho các quy tắc hoặc nghĩa vụ thực tế hằng ngày.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Have to' expresses obligation or necessity. 'Do I have to get up now?' = Is it necessary for me to get up? Present forms: 'I have to go', 'She has to study', 'Do you have to work?' Compared to 'must', 'have to' is commonly used for external rules and everyday necessities.

grammarNotes.title: Cannot find – Expressing inability
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Cannot' (viết tắt: can't) dùng để nói không thể làm gì đó. 'I cannot find my school shoes.' = Tôi không thể tìm thấy giày học. Lưu ý: 'cannot' là một từ, viết liền, không tách thành 'can not' trong văn viết trang trọng. Trong văn nói và văn thông thường dùng 'can't'. Ví dụ: 'I can't see it anywhere.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Cannot' (contraction: can't) expresses inability. 'I cannot find my school shoes' = I am not able to find them. Note: 'cannot' is written as one word in standard written English. In speech and informal writing, 'can't' is used. Example: 'I can't find it anywhere.'

translationVi:
Mẹ: Chào buổi sáng, Tom! Đến giờ thức dậy rồi.
Con: Chào buổi sáng, Mẹ. Con có cần dậy ngay bây giờ không?
Mẹ: Phải! Trường bắt đầu sau một tiếng nữa. Đi rửa mặt trước đi.
Con: Được rồi. Sau đó con có được ăn sáng không?
Mẹ: Tất nhiên rồi. Mẹ đang làm bánh mì nướng và trứng cho con.
Con: Ura! Con thích trứng lắm. Mẹ ơi, đồng phục của con đâu rồi?
Mẹ: Nó ở trên ghế trong phòng con. Mẹ đã ủi nó tối qua rồi.
Con: Cảm ơn Mẹ! Hôm nay trong hộp cơm của con có gì vậy?
Mẹ: Có một cái bánh mì kẹp cá ngừ, ít nho và một hộp nước nhỏ.
Con: Con có thể có thêm một cái bánh quy không?
Mẹ: Được, Mẹ đã để hai cái bánh quy vào túi con rồi. Bây giờ nhanh lên!
Con: Được rồi, được rồi. Mẹ ơi, con không tìm thấy giày học của con.
Mẹ: Giày ở cạnh cửa trước. Con để chúng ở đó hôm qua.
Con: Ồ đúng rồi! Bây giờ con nhớ ra rồi. Con sẵn sàng rồi, Mẹ!
Mẹ: Tốt lắm! Chúc con có một ngày học vui vẻ. Mẹ yêu con.
Con: Con cũng yêu Mẹ. Hẹn gặp lại Mẹ sau!

questions.hint: What name does the mum say first?

questions.explanation: The mum says 'School starts in one hour.'
questions.hint: What time does the mum give?

questions.prompt: What does the mum tell Tom to do first?
questions.explanation: The mum says 'Go and wash your face first.'
questions.hint: What is the first task the mum gives Tom?

questions.prompt: What is the mum making for breakfast?
questions.explanation: The mum says 'I am making toast and eggs for you.'
questions.hint: What two breakfast foods does the mum mention?

options: On the chair in his room
questions.explanation: The mum says 'It is on the chair in your room.'
questions.hint: What piece of furniture is the uniform on?

questions.prompt: When did the mum iron the uniform?
questions.explanation: The mum says 'I ironed it last night.'
questions.hint: What time in the past does the mum mention?

questions.prompt: What is in Tom's lunch box?
options: A cheese sandwich, an apple, and water
A tuna sandwich, grapes, and a juice box
A ham sandwich, biscuits, and milk
A peanut butter sandwich, grapes, and water
questions.explanation: The mum says 'There is a tuna sandwich, some grapes, and a small juice box.'
questions.hint: The mum names three things in the lunch box.

questions.prompt: What extra snack does Tom ask for?
questions.explanation: Tom says 'Can I have a biscuit too?'
questions.hint: What does Tom ask to add to his bag?

questions.explanation: The mum says 'You left them there yesterday.'
questions.hint: Who put the shoes there, and when?

cloze.template: Mum: Good morning, Tom! Time to {{b1}} up. Son: Good morning, Mum. Do I have to get up now? Mum: Yes! School starts in one {{b2}}. Go and wash your face first. Son: Okay. Can I have breakfast after? Mum: Of course. I am making {{b3}} and eggs for you. Son: Yay! I love eggs. Mum, where is my school {{b4}}? Mum: It is on the chair in your room. I ironed it last night. Son: Thank you! What is in my lunch box today? Mum: There is a tuna {{b5}}, some grapes, and a small juice box. Son: Can I have a biscuit too? Mum: Yes, I put two biscuits in your bag. Now {{b6}} up! Son: Okay, okay. Mum, I cannot find my school {{b7}}. Mum: They are by the front {{b8}}. You left them there yesterday. Son: Oh yes! I remember now. I am {{b9}}, Mum! Mum: Great! Have a good day at school. I love you. Son: Love you too, Mum. See you {{b10}}!

blanks.explanation: 'Time to wake up' — the phrase used to tell someone to stop sleeping.
'In one hour' — the time before school begins.
'Toast and eggs' — the breakfast the mum is preparing.
'School uniform' — the clothing worn to school.
'Tuna sandwich' — the main item in Tom's lunch box.
'Hurry up' — telling someone to move faster.
'School shoes' — the footwear Tom cannot find.
'By the front door' — the location where Tom left his shoes.
'I am ready' — Tom announces he is prepared to leave for school.
'See you later' — a common informal goodbye.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Tom's mum wakes him up, makes his breakfast, irons his uniform, and packs his lunch. Do you think children should learn to do some of these things themselves? At what age should a child start taking responsibility for getting ready in the morning?
